SocialVIP — A Linktree-Style Multi-Link Page Built for Taiwan (LINE Pay Tipping)
A friend suggested I revamp my blog footer — instead of a Liker link, add a tip jar. Looking around I found a service called SocialVIP. Pretty handy, so writing it up.

SocialVIP is similar to Linktree — a multi-link page for putting all your social profiles and sites in one place. Linktree was hot for a while because consolidating all your online identities in one URL is genuinely useful.
So why pick SocialVIP over Linktree?
"Because it's localized for Taiwan / Chinese readers."
I think Linktree supports tipping too, but —
, which is the dominant payment method in Taiwan. That makes it work much better with a Taiwan-based audience than Linktree's Stripe/PayPal flow.
The setup is straightforward enough that I won't walk through it step by step — unless someone really wants me to.

Pick an invoice type (Taiwan-specific receipt rules — government-issued unified invoice numbers):
Then redirect to LINE Pay for payment:
Once payment goes through, tipping is complete.
After a while, you'll see the income in your SocialVIP dashboard:
Heads up — minimum withdrawal is 100 (NTD); first withdrawal is special — requires accumulating 200 first.
(Don't forget to set up your bank account, otherwise you can't withdraw.)

A friend suggested adding a tip jar link to my blog footer instead of Liker. Looking around I found SocialVIP — a Linktree-style multi-link page that's localized for Taiwan and supports LINE Pay tipping (which makes it more usable than Linktree for Taiwan-based creators). Short post on the basics — also relevant if you're in a market where LINE Pay matters more than Stripe.
